body_txt: Why bother with Uncanny Family Resemblance , when you can have every member of a family or even complete strangers as the exact same person! Same taste in food, same way they style their hair, same profession, same mannerisms, and they all probably share the same Hive Mind as well. They may even all have the same name. This isn't just a Recurring Character — every town has their own. These are Inexplicably Identical Individuals; there's no reason for them to be perfectly identical — though the series may try to Hand Wave it as them being related — they simply are. Occasionally, they fall into My Species Doth Protest Too Much , having one who's markedly different. The episode often revolves around them. This trope is Truth in Television , but is one that is extremely unlikely to happen, given the vast number of factors at play that decide variation among humans. Theoretically, the random nature of genetic traits means that there could be someone who looks identical to you walking around right now, but there are also a far larger number of people alive today who look similar enough to you that if you saw them, a cursory glance would leave you swearing that you had just seen your Doppelgänger in spite of more obvious differences being apparent if you got a closer look - this likely explains why the prevalence of people claiming to have met an Identical Stranger is likely much higher than the rates of such an occurrence actually happening. Compare and contrast to the game trope of You ALL Look Familiar . Also compare Single-Minded Twins and Only Six Faces . Contrast with the Recurring Extra , where it really is the same character(s) in every town. See also Identical Stranger (where the resemblance is plot important), Clone Army (which is people looking identical because they were created to be that way), and Clone by Conversion (which is when people are modified into replicas of someone else).
